---
title: "Towards translational and mechanistic whole-brain simulations with The Virtual Brain"
subtitle: "Track 4 Workshop — FAIR Brain Data Science Bootcamp"
authors:
  - name: "Leon Stefanovski"
  - name: "Marius Pille"
  - name: "Leon Martin"
affiliations:
  - id: 1
    name: "Charité University Medicine Berlin · Berlin Institute of Health"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    width: 1244
    height: 750
    dpi: 500
    fig-dpi: 500
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    auto-stretch: true
    auto-play-media: true
    chalkboard: true
    incremental: false
    toc: false
    logo: img/Charite.png
    footer: "© Pille, Martin, Stefanovski, Charité Unviersity Medicine Berlin"
    link-external-newwindow: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: github
    include-in-header: _slides-home-btn.html
    css: slides.css
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-iframe: brain-network-background.html
      data-background-color: "#6880af"
      data-background-opacity: "1"
engine: jupyter
jupyter: python3
---

## Welcome {.unnumbered}

- Two-day hands-on workshop on personalized whole-brain modeling with TVB
- Day 1: structured sessions
- Day 2: mini hackathon — bring your own questions / data
- Useful links:
  - <https://thevirtualbrain.org>
  - <https://virtual-twin.github.io/tvbo>
  - <https://virtual-twin.github.io/tvboptim>

## Schedule {.unnumbered}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%" .smaller}
**Day 1**\
**Block 1 - 09:00-12:00:**\
1. Intro to Brain Network Modelling\
2. Defining a FAIR brain network model\
3. Applications in Basic and Clinical Research\
4. Setup & First examples

**Block 2 - 13:00-15:00:**\
5. Bifurcation Theory\
6. Parameter exploration & inference I

**Block 3 - 15:30-17:30:**\
7. Parameter exploration & inference II\
8. Stimulating the brain

:::
::: {.column width="40%" .smaller}
**Day 2**\
**Block 1 - 09:00-11:30:**\
Mini hackathon - Bring your own data / research question\
Plotting the Brain


**Block 2 - 12:00-13:30:**\
Mini hackathon - Bring your own data / research question\
Plotting the Brain

:::
:::

# 1. Intro to Brain Network Modelling {background-color="#6880af"}
- TODO: motivation for whole-brain network modeling
- TODO: brief historical arc of TVB

## Basic ideas and history

:::: {.rows}

::: {.row height="70%"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="25%"}
![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow1.png)
:::

::: {.column width="25%" .fragment}
![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow2.png)
:::

::: {.column width="25%" .fragment}
![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow3.png)
:::

::: {.column width="25%" .fragment}
![](img/figures/basic_idea_imaging_workflow4.png)
:::

::::

:::

::: {.row height="30%" .fragment}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}
![](img/state_evolution_node.png){height=150px fig-align=center}

:::

::: {.column}
```{dot}
//| fig-align: center
//| fig-width: 5
//| fig-height: 1.6
digraph G {
  rankdir=LR;
  bgcolor="transparent";
  size="5,1.6!";
  ratio=compress;
  nodesep=0.25;
  ranksep=0.35;
  node [fontname="Helvetica", fontsize=14, fontcolor="black", style="filled,rounded", penwidth=2, margin="0.08,0.04"];
  edge [penwidth=1.2, arrowsize=0.7];

  S     [label="State: S(t)", shape=box,     fillcolor="#d6e4f0", color="#6880af"];
  F     [label="Evolution Function: f(S, θ)", shape=box, fillcolor="#6880af", color="#3a5080", fontcolor="white"];
  dS    [label="Next State: dS/dt", shape=box, fillcolor="#d6e4f0", color="#6880af"];
  theta [label="Parameters: θ", shape=ellipse, fillcolor="#f0dfd6", color="#af8068"];

  S     -> F;
  theta -> F;
  F     -> dS;
}
```
:::
::::

:::

::::


## Mathematical framework

$$
dS_i = \left[f_d(S_i, \theta^d, C_i, I_i) \right]dt + g(S_i, \theta^g)\, dW_i
$$


::: {.fragment}

**State Evolution**

- $S_i$ - state variables at node $i$
- $f_d$ - dynamics function with parameters $\theta^d$
- $C_i$ - coupling input from connected nodes
- $I_i$ - external input (stimulation, driving signals)
- $g$ - noise diffusion coefficient with parameters $\theta^g$
- $dW_i$ - Wiener process (stochastic fluctuations)
:::


## Dynamical Systems


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column style="font-size: 0.55em;"}

A mass $m$ on a spring with stiffness $k$. <br/>
Perpetual oscillation at $\omega = \sqrt{k/m}$.

**State Equations:**
$$
\dot{x} = v
$$
$$
\dot{v} = - \frac{k}{m}*x
$$

**State Variables**

| **Variable** | **Initial Value** | **Unit** | **Description** |
|--------------|-------------------|----------|-----------------|
| $x$ | 2.0 | $\mathrm{m}$ | Displacement from equilibrium |
| $v$ | 0.0 | $\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{s}}$ | Velocity |

**Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Value** | **Unit** | **Description** |
|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------------|
| $k$ | 0.0001 | $\frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{m}}$ | Spring stiffness constant |
| $m$ | 1.0 | $\mathrm{kg}$ | Mass of the oscillating body |


:::

::: {.column}

::: {.r-stack}

![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_single_thumb.png){fig-align="center"}

![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_single.gif){.fragment fig-align="center"}
:::
:::

::::

## Initial Conditions {background-color="#ffffff"}


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column style="font-size: 0.55em;"}

A mass $m$ on a spring with stiffness $k$. <br/>
Perpetual oscillation at $\omega = \sqrt{k/m}$.

**State Equations:**
$$
\dot{x} = v
$$
$$
\dot{v} = - \frac{k}{m}*x
$$

**State Variables**

| **Variable** | **Initial Value** | **Unit** | **Description** |
|--------------|-------------------|----------|-----------------|
| $x$ | 2.0 | $\mathrm{m}$ | Displacement from equilibrium |
| $v$ | 0.0 | $\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{s}}$ | Velocity |

**Parameters**

| **Parameter** | **Value** | **Unit** | **Description** |
|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------------|
| $k$ | 0.0001 | $\frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{m}}$ | Spring stiffness constant |
| $m$ | 1.0 | $\mathrm{kg}$ | Mass of the oscillating body |
:::

::: {.column}

::: {.r-stack}
![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_ics_thumb.png){fig-align="center"}

![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_ics.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" }
:::
:::

::::

## Phase Space {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_phase_space.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"}

## Parameters — Mass {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {.r-stack}
![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_mass_thumb.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"}

![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_mass.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"}
:::


## Towards Realism — Damping & Load {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {.r-stack}
![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_realism_thumb.png){fig-align="center" height="580px"}

![](img/figures/dynamical_systems/fig-dynamical_systems_springs_realism.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height="580px"}
:::

# Mean Field Models {background-video="img/videos/birds.mp4" background-video-loop="true" background-video-muted="true"}


## Mean field models & Neural Masses

![](img/mean_field_overview.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}

- Population rate: $r(t) = \langle \#\text{spikes}\rangle / (N\,\Delta t)$
- Jansen-Rit (3 populations): PC = $y_1 - y_2$, the EEG-like PSP at pyramidal cells

## EEG-Like Activity with the Jansen-Rit-Model

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="65%" style="font-size: 0.45em; height: 660px; overflow-y: scroll;"}

**JansenRit1995**
Lumped-parameter cortical column model with three neural populations: pyramidal cells, excitatory interneurons, and inhibitory interneurons. Each population is modeled by a PSP block (2nd-order linear filter) and a sigmoidal potential-to-firing-rate transformation.

**Derived Variables**
$$
v_{pyr} = y_{1} - y_{2}
$$

**State Equations**
$$
\dot{y_{0}} = y_{3}
$$
$$
\dot{y_{3}} = - y_{0}*a^{2} - 2*a*y_{3} + A*a*\operatorname{Sigm}{\left(y_{1} - y_{2} \right)}
$$
$$
\dot{y_{1}} = y_{4}
$$
$$
\dot{y_{4}} = - y_{1}*a^{2} - 2*a*y_{4} + A*a*\left(p + C_{2}*\operatorname{Sigm}{\left(C_{1}*y_{0} \right)} + K*c_{intercolumn}\right)
$$
$$
\dot{y_{2}} = y_{5}
$$
$$
\dot{y_{5}} = - y_{2}*b^{2} - 2*b*y_{5} + B*C_{4}*b*\operatorname{Sigm}{\left(C_{3}*y_{0} \right)}
$$

with
$$
\operatorname{Sigm}{\left(v \right)} = \frac{2*e_{0}}{1 + e^{r*\left(v_{0} - v\right)}}
$$

*State Variables*

| **Variable** | **Initial Value** | **Unit** | **Description** |
|--------------|-------------------|----------|-----------------|
| $y_{0}$ | 0.1 | $\mathrm{mV}$ |  |
| $y_{3}$ | 0.1 | $\frac{\mathrm{mV}}{\mathrm{s}}$ |  |
| $y_{1}$ | 0.1 | $\mathrm{mV}$ |  |
| $y_{4}$ | 0.1 | $\frac{\mathrm{mV}}{\mathrm{s}}$ |  |
| $y_{2}$ | 0.1 | $\mathrm{mV}$ |  |
| $y_{5}$ | 0.1 | $\frac{\mathrm{mV}}{\mathrm{s}}$ |  |

*Parameters*

| **Parameter** | **Value** | **Unit** | **Description** |
|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------------|
| $A$ | 3.25 | $\mathrm{mV}$ | Maximum amplitude of excitatory PSP (EPSP) |
| $B$ | 22 | $\mathrm{mV}$ | Maximum amplitude of inhibitory PSP (IPSP) |
| $C$ | 135 | — | Lumped connectivity constant (C = C1) |
| $a$ | 100 | $\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ | Reciprocal of EPSP time constant |
| $b$ | 50 | $\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ | Reciprocal of IPSP time constant |
| $v_{0}$ | 6 | $\mathrm{mV}$ | PSP for 50% firing rate (sigmoid midpoint) |
| $e_{0}$ | 2.5 | $\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ | Half of maximum firing rate |
| $r$ | 0.56 | $\mathrm{mV}^{-1}$ | Steepness of sigmoid transformation |
| $p$ | 220 | $\mathrm{s}^{-1}$ | Mean external input (afferent pulse density) |
| $K$ | 0 | — | Per-node coupling strength scaling (K[i] scales coupling received by node i) |


**Derived Parameters**

$$
C_{1} = C
$$
$$
C_{2} = 0.8*C
$$
$$
C_{3} = 0.25*C
$$
$$
C_{4} = 0.25*C
$$


:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](img/figures/jansen1995/fig-jr-singlenode.png){width="100%"}
:::

::::


## Network dynamics

```{python}
from tvbo import Network
net = Network.from_string("""
label: Visual Pathway

nodes:
  - {id: 0, label: V1}
  - {id: 1, label: V2}
  - {id: 2, label: MT}

edges:
  - source: 0
    target: 1
    parameters:
      weight:
        value: 0.7
  - source: 1
    target: 2
    parameters:
      weight:
        value: 0.4
  - source: 0
    target: 2
    parameters:
      weight:
        value: 0.2
""")

net.plot_overview()
```


## Uncoupled system

<div style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0; width:100%; height:660px;">
<div style="flex:0 0 28%; box-sizing:border-box; overflow-y:scroll; height:100%; font-size:0.48em; padding-right:6px;">

**@Kuramoto1975**

Phase oscillator model. Each node evolves with its own natural frequency $\omega$; coupling $I$ is added by the network.

**State Equations**
$$\dot{\theta} = \omega$$

*State Variables*

| Variable | Initial value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| $\theta$ | $\sim\mathcal{U}(0,2\pi)$ | — |

**Derived Variables**
$$activity = \sin(\theta)$$

*Parameters*

| Parameter | Value | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| $\omega$ | 1.0 | — | Natural frequency |

*Simulation settings*

| | |
|---|---|
| Duration | 100 s |
| Step size | 0.01 s |
| Integrator | Heun |
| Nodes | 3 (V1, V2, MT) |
| Coupling $K$ | 0.0 (uncoupled) |

</div>
<div style="flex:1; box-sizing:border-box; padding-left:8px;">

![](img/videos/NetworkDynamics/uncoupled_kuramoto.gif){width="100%"}

</div>
</div>

## Coupled system {.nostretch}

<div style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0; width:100%; height:660px;">
<div style="flex:0 0 28%; box-sizing:border-box; overflow-y:scroll; height:100%; font-size:0.48em; padding-right:6px;">

**@Kuramoto1975** — same model as before, now with coupling enabled.

**State Equations**
$$\dot{\theta} = I + \omega$$

**Coupling: $I$**

$$
I = \frac{K \sum_{j=0}^{-1 + N} - {g}_{i,j} \sin{\left(\theta_{i} - \theta_{j} \right)}}{3}
$$

Receives $\theta$ from connected regions.

**Pre-synaptic:** $c_{\text{pre}} = \sin(\theta_j - \theta_i)$

**Post-synaptic:** $c_{\text{post}} = \dfrac{K \cdot gx}{3}$

*Coupling parameters*

| Parameter | Value | Description |
|---|---|---|
| $K$ | 0.4 | Global coupling strength |

*Network*

| Edge | Weight |
|---|---|
| V1 → V2 | 0.7 |
| V2 → MT | 0.4 |
| V1 → MT | 0.2 |

*Simulation settings*

| | |
|---|---|
| Duration | 100 s |
| Step size | 0.01 s |
| Integrator | Heun |

</div>
<div style="flex:1; box-sizing:border-box; padding-left:8px;">

![](img/videos/NetworkDynamics/coupled_kuramoto.gif){width="100%"}

</div>
</div>

## Coupled Brain Network
<!-- - TODO: nodes, coupling, delays, noise
- TODO: connectome loading
- TODO: global coupling sweep -->

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=20%}
![](img/fig-network_visualization_rows.png){height=590px}
:::

::: {.column width=80%}

::: {.fragment fragment-index=2}
$$
C_i = f_c^{\text{post}}\Big(\sum_j A_{ij}\, f_c^{\text{pre}}(S_i, S_j(t-\tau_{ij}), \theta^c), S_i, \theta^c\Big)
$$

::: {style="font-size: 0.55em;"}
**Coupling**

- $f_c^{\text{pre}}$ - pre-aggregation transformation
- $A_{ij}$ - structural connectivity weight ($j \to i$)
- $\tau_{ij}$ - transmission delay (tract length / conduction speed)
- $f_c^{\text{post}}$ - post-aggregation transformation
- $\theta^c$ - coupling parameters

:::

:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index=3}
![](img/coupling_schematic.png){height="250px" fig-align="center"}
:::

:::

::::

# 2. Defining a FAIR brain network model {background-color="#6880af"}

## Why FAIR? TVB-O in one slide

![](img/tvbo/tvbo-network-insets.png)

## Why FAIR? TVB-O in one slide

```{=html}
<style>
.se-dia {
  font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
  background: linear-gradient(145deg, #f4f8f8, #e4edef);
  border: 2px solid #9ab5b8; border-radius: 18px;
  padding: 20px 22px 18px; width: 100%; box-sizing:border-box;
  margin: 0 auto; box-shadow: 0 6px 32px rgba(43,122,120,.1);
}
.se-dia .hdr { text-align:center; font-size:1.15em; font-weight:700; color:#2b6b6b; letter-spacing:.03em; margin-bottom:12px; }
.se-dia .top { display:grid; grid-template-columns:3fr 2fr; grid-template-rows:auto auto; gap:10px; margin-bottom:12px; }
.se-dia .top .net { grid-row:1/3; }
.se-dia .bot { display:flex; gap:10px; }
.se-dia .g { border:1.5px dashed; border-radius:12px; padding:10px 12px 12px; min-width:0; display:flex; flex-direction:column; }
.se-dia .gl { font-size:.54em; font-weight:700; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.09em; margin-bottom:6px; padding-left:2px; }
.se-dia .pp { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:5px 6px; align-items:center; }
.se-dia .p  { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; padding:4px 10px; border-radius:16px; font-size:.52em; font-weight:500; white-space:nowrap; border:1.2px solid; line-height:1.3; }
.se-dia .sub { border-radius:9px; padding:7px 10px 8px; margin-top:7px; }
.se-dia .sub .gl { font-size:.48em; margin-bottom:3px; }
.se-dia .f1  { flex:1; }
.se-dia .f08 { flex:.8; }
.se-dia .arr { font-size:.48em; color:#6a9a9a; margin:0 2px; line-height:1; font-weight:300; }
.se-dia .p.opt { border-style:dashed; opacity:.7; }
/* Network: blue */
.g.c-net        { border-color:#8bbde0; background:rgba(200,225,240,.25); }
.g.c-net > .gl  { color:#2e6e9c; }
.p.c-net        { background:#9dd0f0; border-color:#5ea8d4; color:#1a4a6e; }
.g.c-net .sub        { border:1.2px solid #a8d0e8; background:rgba(210,230,245,.35); }
.g.c-net .sub .gl    { color:#4a8ab8; }
.p.c-dyn        { background:#bee0f4; border-color:#7ec0e0; color:#1e5a80; }
.p.c-cpl        { background:#d0e8f6; border-color:#98cce6; color:#2a6890; }
/* Integration: teal */
.g.c-int        { border-color:#7abab8; background:rgba(180,220,218,.25); }
.g.c-int > .gl  { color:#2b6b6b; }
.p.c-int        { background:#8ad0cc; border-color:#52a8a4; color:#1a4e4e; }
/* Event: amber */
.g.c-evt        { border-color:#d4c4a0; background:rgba(230,218,195,.25); }
.g.c-evt > .gl  { color:#8a6a30; }
.p.c-evt        { background:#e8d4b0; border-color:#c8a870; color:#5a4420; }
.g.c-evt .sub        { border:1.2px solid #d8c8a8; background:rgba(235,225,205,.35); }
.g.c-evt .sub .gl    { color:#9a7a40; }
.p.c-stm        { background:#f0e0c4; border-color:#d4b888; color:#6a5030; }
/* Observation: cyan-teal */
.g.c-obs        { border-color:#88c4c4; background:rgba(190,225,225,.25); }
.g.c-obs > .gl  { color:#2e7070; }
.p.c-obs        { background:#98d4d0; border-color:#5eb0ac; color:#1a5050; }
/* Exploration: green */
.g.c-exp        { border-color:#98c8a4; background:rgba(200,230,210,.25); }
.g.c-exp > .gl  { color:#3a7048; }
.p.c-exp        { background:#a8d8b4; border-color:#70b480; color:#1e4a2a; }
/* Analysis: slate blue */
.g.c-ana        { border-color:#98a8c4; background:rgba(200,210,228,.25); }
.g.c-ana > .gl  { color:#3a5070; }
.p.c-ana        { background:#b0c0d8; border-color:#7898b8; color:#1e3450; }
/* Environment: warm gray */
.g.c-env        { border-color:#b0b8b8; background:rgba(215,220,220,.25); }
.g.c-env > .gl  { color:#505858; }
.p.c-env        { background:#c4cccc; border-color:#94a0a0; color:#2a3434; }
</style>
<div class="se-dia">
  <div class="hdr">SimulationExperiment</div>
  <div class="top">
    <div class="g c-net net">
      <div class="gl">Network</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-net opt">Parcellation</span>
        <span class="arr">⟶</span>
        <span class="p c-net">Node</span>
        <span class="arr">⟶</span>
        <span class="p c-net">Edge</span>
      </div>
      <div class="sub">
        <div class="gl">Dynamics</div>
        <div class="pp">
          <span class="p c-dyn">StateVariable</span>
          <span class="p c-dyn">Parameter</span>
          <span class="p c-dyn">Equation</span>
          <span class="p c-dyn">DerivedVariable</span>
          <span class="p c-dyn">DerivedParameter</span>
          <span class="p c-dyn">Function</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="sub">
        <div class="gl">Coupling</div>
        <div class="pp">
          <span class="p c-cpl">Parameter</span>
          <span class="p c-cpl">Equation</span>
          <span class="p c-cpl">IncomingState</span>
          <span class="p c-cpl">LocalState</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="g c-int">
      <div class="gl">Integration</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-int">Method</span>
        <span class="p c-int">Noise</span>
        <span class="p c-int">InitialCondition</span>
        <span class="p c-int">TimeSeries</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="g c-evt">
      <div class="gl">Event</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-evt">Equation</span>
        <span class="p c-evt">Target</span>
      </div>
      <div class="sub">
        <div class="gl">Stimulus</div>
        <div class="pp">
          <span class="p c-stm">Waveform</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="bot">
    <div class="g c-obs f1">
      <div class="gl">Observation</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-obs">Source</span>
        <span class="p c-obs">Pipeline</span>
        <span class="arr">⟶</span>
        <span class="p c-obs">DataSource</span>
        <span class="p c-obs">DerivedObs.</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="g c-exp f08">
      <div class="gl">Exploration</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-exp">Exploration</span>
        <span class="p c-exp">Algorithm</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="g c-ana f08">
      <div class="gl">Analysis</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-ana">Optimization</span>
        <span class="p c-ana">Continuation</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="g c-env f1">
      <div class="gl">Environment</div>
      <div class="pp">
        <span class="p c-env">SoftwareEnv.</span>
        <span class="p c-env">Execution</span>
        <span class="p c-env">Reference</span>
        <span class="p c-env">Author</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Model Specification with TVB-O

:::: {.columns}


::: {.column width=35%}

**Metadata Schema**
```yaml
id: 21
model: Dynamics
dynamics:
  name: Dynamics
  parameters:
    a:
      name: a
      value: 1.0
  state_variables:
    x:
      name: x
      equation:
        rhs: a*x
      initial_value: 1.0
integration:
  method: Heun
  time_scale: ms
  duration: 4.0
  step_size: 0.01
explorations:
  name: a_sweep
  space:
  - parameter: a
    explored_values:
    - 1.0
    - 0.0
    - -1.0
```
:::

::: {.column width=65%}

**Python API**
```python
from tvbo import DynamicalSystem, SimulationExperiment
from tvbo.datamodel.schema import Exploration, ExplorationAxis

bsplot.style.use("tvbo")

g = DynamicalSystem(
    state_variables={"x": {
                          "equation": {"rhs": "a*x"},
                          "initial_value": 1.0}},
    parameters={"a": {"value": 1.0}},
)

exp = SimulationExperiment(dynamics=g)

exp.explorations = Exploration(
    name="a_sweep",
    space=ExplorationAxis(
      parameter="a",
      explored_values=[1.0, 0, -1.0]
      ),
)

exp.integration.duration = 4.0
exp.integration.step_size = 0.01

res = exp.run("tvboptim")
```
:::


::::

## Applications in basic research

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

![](img/figures/applications/Schirner2023Title.png)

![[@Schirner2023]](img/videos/Schirner2023videosnippet.mp4){width="100%" loop="" muted=""}

:::

::: {.column}

![](img/figures/applications/Koller2024Title.png)

![[@Koller2024]](img/videos/travelling_waves.mp4){width="100%" loop="" muted=""}

:::

::::

## Clinical applications

- TODO: translational use-cases
- TODO: virtual epileptic / Alzheimer / stroke patient examples

# Setup & First Examples


# 3. Bifurcation Analysis {background-color="#6880af"}

## How to analyse a brain system

![](img/bifurcation_overview.png){fig-align="center" width="96%"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%" .smaller}
**Time series**
$x(t)$ at fixed $(a, x_0)$
:::
::: {.column width="33%" .smaller}
**Phase plane**
flow geometry at fixed $a$
:::
::: {.column width="34%" .smaller}
**Bifurcation diagram**
regimes as $a$ varies
:::
:::

## Linear Systems


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%" .r-fit-text}
$$
\dot x = a\,x \quad\Rightarrow\quad x(t) = x_0\,e^{a t}
$$

- $x(t)$: State variable
- $x_0$: Initial condition
- $a$: Parameter

Solution:
$$
x(t) = x_0\,e^{a t}
$$

Equilibrium:
$$
\dot{x}(t) = ax(t) = 0?
$$
$$
\quad\Rightarrow\quad x_{eq} = 0
$$


:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](img/figures/bifurcation/fig-linear_system_a1_and_a_minus1.png)
:::


::::

## 2D portraits

![](img/figures/bifurcation/fig-bifurcation-phase-portraits-animation.gif){fig-align="center" width="82%"}

::: {.smaller}
**Same local rule:** arrows define the flow; trajectories reveal attraction, repulsion, rotation and separatrices.
:::

## Regime-change building blocks

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column .smaller width=20%}

- Top: equilibria as $a$ varies.
- Bottom: local flow $\dot x=f(x)$ at the dotted parameter slice.

:::

::: {.column width=80%}
![](img/normal_forms.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::

::::


## Hopf: birth of an oscillation

$$
\dot z = (a + i\omega)z - |z|^2z,
\qquad r_{\mathrm{cycle}} = \sqrt a
$$

![](img/hopf_birth.gif){fig-align="center" width="100%"}

::: {.smaller}
fixed point loses stability $\Rightarrow$ stable oscillation appears
:::


## Generic2dOscillator: regimes over input $I$

![](img/g2d_bifurcation.png){fig-align="center" width="82%"}

::: {.smaller}
resting branch | oscillatory window | folds and Hopf points
:::


## Numerical continuation {.r-fit-text}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="62%"}
![](img/continuation_pseudo_arclength_white.png)
:::
::: {.column width="38%" .smaller}
$$
F(x, a)=0,
\qquad (x,a)=(x(s),a(s))
$$

- follow branches through folds
- detect folds and Hopf points
- switch to periodic orbits

:::
:::


## TVB simulation infused with bifurcation analysis

![](img/g2d_network_bifurcation.gif){fig-align="center" width="100%"}

::: {.smaller}
TVBO + tvboptim simulation of Generic2dOscillator on the Desikan-Killiany network. Nodes are colored by their instantaneous $V(t)$ and projected onto axial (top) and sagittal views; the regime map places each node at its current $(I_{\mathrm{eff}}, V)$ on the bifurcation diagram.
:::


## 3.1 Bifurcation analysis: hands-on

- Notebook: [`notebooks/03_bifurcation_analysis.qmd`](notebooks/03_bifurcation_analysis.qmd)
- Sweep input $I$ in a TVB neural mass model
- Continue fixed points and periodic orbits
- Read the diagram as a **map of regimes**

```python
result = exp.run("bifurcationkit.jl")
```



{{< include _slides-section-4.qmd >}}

<!-- section 4 lives in _slides-section-4.qmd to keep diffs scoped and
     reduce merge conflicts when collaborators touch slides.qmd -->


# 5. Stimulating the brain {background-color="#6880af"}

## Visual evoked potentials

[@Jansen1995]

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width="30%"}
**Original**

![](img/figures/jansen1995/original/fig_11.png)
::::

:::: {.column width="40%"}
**Specification**

```yaml {code-line-numbers="|1-18|19-36|37-54|55-72|73-87"}
- id: 6
  label: "VEP: different columns, with delay
          (Fig. 11)"
  dynamics:
    name: JansenRit1995_Delayed_VEP
    parameters:
      A: {value: 3.25, unit: mV}
      B: {value: 22, unit: mV}
      C: {value: 135}
      a: {value: 100, unit: s^-1}
      b: {value: 50, unit: s^-1}
      v0: {value: 6, unit: mV}
      e0: {value: 2.5, unit: s^-1}
      r: {value: 0.56, unit: mV^-1}
      p:
        value: 220
        unit: s^-1
        shape: "(n_nodes,)"
        distribution: *dist_p_uniform
      a_d: {value: 30, unit: s^-1}
      K: {value: 0, shape: "(n_nodes,)"}
    derived_parameters: *jr_derived
    functions: *jr_functions
    coupling_terms: *jr_coupling
    state_variables:
      y0: {equation: {rhs: y3}}
      y3:
        equation:
          rhs: >-
            A*a*Sigm(y1 - y2)
            - 2*a*y3 - a**2*y0
      y1: {equation: {rhs: y4}}
      y4:
        equation:
          rhs: >-
            A*a*(p + P
            + C2*Sigm(C1*y0) + z0)
            - 2*a*y4 - a**2*y1
      y2: {equation: {rhs: y5}}
      y5:
        equation:
          rhs: >-
            B*b*(C4*Sigm(C3*y0))
            - 2*b*y5 - b**2*y2
      z0: {equation: {rhs: z1}}
      z1:
        equation:
          rhs: >-
            A*a_d*K*c_intercolumn
            - 2*a_d*z1 - a_d**2*z0
    derived_variables:
      v_pyr: {equation: {rhs: y1 - y2}}
    output: [v_pyr]
  network:
    number_of_regions: 2
    number_of_nodes: 2
    conduction_speed: {value: 0}
    nodes:
      - {id: 0}
      - id: 1
        parameters:
          B: {value: 17.6}
          C: {value: 108}
    edges:
      - source: 0
        target: 1
        directed: true
      - source: 1
        target: 0
        directed: true
    coupling: *sigmoidal_coupling
  events: *flash_events
  integration: *integration_vep
  execution:
    random_seed: 42
  explorations:
    VEP_delayed_fig11:
      mode: zip
      n_trials: 40
      average: trials
      parameters:
        K:
          element_domains:
            - element: 0
              explored_values: [300, 100]
            - element: 1
              explored_values: [1000, 4000]
```
::::

:::: {.column width="30%"}
**Reproduced**

![](img/figures/jansen1995/reproduced/Jansen1995_Fig11.png)
::::

:::::

<!-- - TODO: stimulation primitives in TVB
- TODO: VEP / TEP example -->

# Day 2 — Mini Hackathon {background-color="#6880af"}

## Format

- TODO: pitch your question (5 min each)
- TODO: form small groups
- TODO: checkpoint + wrap-up times

## Bring your own…

- TODO: data formats we can support
- TODO: EBRAINS / local setup reminder

# Wrap-up {background-color="#6880af"}

## Take-aways

- TODO: what to remember from Day 1
- TODO: where to go next

## Thanks & contact {.r-fit-text}

::::{.rows}

:::{.row}
![Your Feedback](img/survey_qr.png){fig-align=center height=200px}
:::

:::{.row}
Contact
:::

:::{.row}
| ![](img/Charite.png){fig-alt="Charite logo" width=30%} | ![](img/BIH.png){fig-alt="BIH logo" width=30%} | ![](img/BSS.png){fig-alt="BSS logo" width=30%} |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
:::

::::

## References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::

## {.unnumbered}
